This family is part of the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants like mint and figwort. intro: Gasteranthus acropodus, also known as the 'Antirrhinum' or 'Bearded Trumpet', is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Gasteranthus acropodus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
